quotes.text: The Hebrew meaning of the construction translated "dwelt careless" means "living at ease.
quotes.speaker: Daniel H. Ludlow
quotes.source: A Companion to Your Study of the Old Testament
quotes.attr: Daniel H. Ludlow, A Companion to Your Study of the Old Testament, p.211

quotes.text: The word "still" has been translated from a Hebrew wording meaning "slack," "quiet," or "inactive.
quotes.speaker: Daniel H. Ludlow
quotes.source: A Companion to Your Study of the Old Testament
quotes.attr: Daniel H. Ludlow, A Companion to Your Study of the Old Testament, p.211

quotes.text: The Hebrew translated "and saluted him" could have been translated "and asked for his peace.
quotes.speaker: Daniel H. Ludlow
quotes.source: A Companion to Your Study of the Old Testament
quotes.attr: Daniel H. Ludlow, A Companion to Your Study of the Old Testament, p.211

notes: Violation of the Levite's concubine (Judges 19) a concubine leaves her home in Ephraim and goes to her father's home (19:1-2) b happy negotiations for the woman (19:3-4) o Levite comes and speaks kindly to his concubine and her father, and he wins her back o tranquil scene of father & husband peaceably conversing (about concubine?) c hospitality in Bethlehem (19:5-9) o the Levite is detained extra days by his hospitable host d TURNING POINT: Levite begins journey home; ironically refuses to stay overnight in Jebus, because it is "a town of foreigners" (19:10-13) c' inhospitality in Gibeah (19:14-21) o local people do not take the Levite and his concubine in; finally taken in by an outsider, who fears for their safety b' terrible negotiations for the woman (19:22-26) o evil men of Gibeah come and speak hostilely to the old man who is hosting the Levite and his concubine; they prevail & violate concubine a' concubine is brought back to her home in Ephraim-dead (19:27-30) adapted from David A. Dorsey, The Literary Structure of the Old Testament
